title: Neurophyllodes tridens
taxonomy: Neurophyllodes tridens is a genus of flowering plants in the family Orchidaceae. It is a monotypic genus, containing a single species. Neurophyllodes tridens is native to Bolivia. The classification of Neurophyllodes tridens places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification within the subfamily is likely to be in the tribe Cranichideae.

intro: Neurophyllodes tridens, also known as "Three-toothed Cordia" or "Mexican Plum," is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Neurophyllodes tridens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Neurophyllodes tridens is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, pain, and skin conditions.
